title: India’s polyester raw materials rise on firm feedstock trends
summary:
Polyester feedstock prices strengthened, driven by steady gains in PTA across India and China.
Crude oil-linked cost support remained firm amid ongoing Middle East tensions, sustaining upstream momentum.
However, MEG trends were mixed, reflecting uneven cost dynamics within the value chain.
Despite this, cautious downstream demand is keeping the market largely range-bound with a mild positive bias.

title: India-New Zealand FTA to boost textile exports: CITI
summary:
India’s textile and apparel exports are set to gain from the India–New Zealand FTA, which ensures duty-free access and supports market diversification.
New Zealand imported Indian made-up textiles worth NZ $80.22 million in 2025.
The pact is expected to drive value-added exports and aid India’s $350 billion sector target by 2030.

title: India, New Zealand to sign FTA today
summary:
India and New Zealand will sign an FTA today that will remove tariffs on all of the former’s exports to the latter, and either sharply reduce or remove tariffs on 95 per cent of imports from the latter.
Talks began in March 2025, after a hiatus of ten years.
India feels the FTA will open new export opportunities for traditional and MSME-driven sectors, particularly in leather, handloom and handicrafts.

title: Germany's business sentiment hits 4-year low in April: ifo
summary:
Germany’s ifo Business Climate Index fell to 84.4 in April 2026 from 86.3 in March, its lowest since May 2020, signalling deteriorating business sentiment. 
Firms reported weaker expectations and current conditions amid crisis in the Middle East. 
Manufacturing saw pessimism rise despite slightly better conditions. 
Services and trade declined sharply.

title: Is Africa the emerging leader in sustainable fashion innovation?
summary:
Two African innovators from Uganda and Tanzania are amongst the Top 20 finalists of the 2026 Global Change Award, highlighting Africa’s growing influence in sustainable fashion. 
Their breakthroughs—carbon-capturing textile dyes and seaweed-based bio-leather—showcase Africa’s potential to deliver scalable, regenerative, and eco-friendly fashion solutions.

title: Bangladesh home textile exports fall 47% in Q1
summary:
Bangladesh’s home textile exports fell 47.8 per cent YoY to $170.52 million in Q1 2026, reflecting sharp demand slowdown across major markets.
US imports dropped 44 per cent but remained the largest destination.
The decline highlights inventory correction, cautious sourcing, and weakening demand across the US and Europe despite stable annual export trends.

title: Vietnam’s economy maintains strong momentum amid uncertainties: AMRO
summary:
Vietnam’s economy has demonstrated notable resilience despite continuing global uncertainties, as per AMRO 
After an exceptionally strong 2025 growth performance, its growth is expected to soften to around 7.2 per cent in 2026-27. 
Safeguarding macro-financial stability will require a prudent, well-calibrated policy mix that avoids procyclicality, while managing emerging macro‑financial pressures.

title: Adverse impact of Iran war may hit India largely in Q1 FY27: ICRA
summary:
The YoY growth in India’s economic activity, as measured by the ICRA business activity monitor, eased to a five-month low of 7.6 per cent in March this year from 10 per cent in February 2026.
ICRA pegs India’s GDP growth at 7 per cent in Q4 FY26.
A large part of the adverse impact of the Middle East crisis is likely to manifest in Q1 FY27.

title: Finalise Bangladesh’s textile-RMG circular economy strategy: Experts
summary:
Bangladesh government officials, industry leaders and sustainability experts recently called for finalising a national circular economy strategy for the textile and RMG sector as that is essential to protect competitiveness in the global apparel market. 
They emphasised the need to embed circular practices across the entire value chain while improving transparency and building institutional capacity.

title: UNCTAD, Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore launch partnership
summary:
UNCTAD and the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore have launched a partnership to support the transition toward more sustainable, resilient and inclusive maritime transport systems. 
They will promote adoption of alternative fuels and digital solutions across ports and shipping networks. 
Efforts will focus on approaches that can be adapted to different national contexts.

title: New Zealand’s apparel imports dip slightly to $1.18 bn in FY2025–26
summary:
New Zealand’s apparel imports declined 1.5 per cent in FY2025-26, reflecting cautious sourcing and inventory correction, while fabric imports surged 24.2 per cent, signalling steady input demand.
March saw a rebound in imports and exports.
Fabric exports rose 33.2 per cent annually, indicating strengthening momentum in specialised textile segments.

title: BKMEA concerned over load shedding, rising energy costs in Bangladesh
summary:
Expressing concern over the ongoing load shedding and fuel crisis, the Bangladesh Knitwear Manufacturers and Exporters Association has said production is being severely disrupted due to power shortage, hitting exports. 
The crisis is especially acute in some rural areas like Bhaluka, Sripur and Rajendrapur, and many factories are running at 50-60-per cent capacity.

title: China dominates Turkiye in baby garment exports, unit prices
summary:
China continues to dominate global baby garment exports over Turkiye in both value and volume, with higher unit prices reflecting stronger value-added positioning and supply chain strength.
While China’s exports remain relatively stable, Turkiye has seen declining volumes despite some price gains, underscoring competitive pressures and evolving demand trends.

title: Egypt’s SCZONE signs $16.5-mn pact with Turkish firm for RMG project
summary:
The Suez Canal Economic Zone has signed a $16.5-million pact with Turkish firm ELA Tekstil to set up a RMG and denim factory in the Qantara West Industrial Zone. 
The project, expected to create around 2,500 direct jobs, will produce nearly 7 million pieces of RMG and denim products annually, with four-fifths of output meant for exports. 
This project brings the total number of projects in the Zone to 42.

title: Norway sees steady apparel imports at $389 mn in Jan–Feb period
summary:
Norway’s apparel imports remained stable at $389.96 million in January-February 2026.
Unisex garments led demand, with trousers and shorts topping categories.
Cotton and man-made fibres dominated materials, while woven garments held a higher share than knits.
China remained the top supplier, Bangladesh steady, and Turkiye’s share declined, indicating a slight sourcing shift.

title: Structural issues from higher military RMG localisation: S Korean SMEs
summary:
South Korean textile sector SMEs are concerned that the increased localisation of military apparel is leading to structural issues like cost burdens and procurement risks, which are disproportionately hitting small sewing firms. 
The textile industry committee of the Korea Federation of SMEs has sought adjustment official estimate prices and establishment of a risk-sharing structure for fabric procurement.

title: Oversized, UV-ready & earth-toned: Sportswear trends 2026
summary:
China’s sportswear trends are led by oversized silhouettes and core colours, with rising demand driven by upcoming global sporting events. 
Jersey exports are surging, reflecting strong market growth. 
While oversized styles remain relevant, the focus is shifting to lightweight, structured, and comfortable fabrics, alongside growing demand for versatile and sun-protective apparel.

title: Japan’s apparel imports rise 2.8% to $24.3 bn in FY 2025-26
summary:
Japan’s apparel imports rose 2.8 per cent to ~$24.36 billion in FY 2025–26, reflecting steady demand despite global uncertainties, though March imports fell 3.9 per cent YoY.
Textile yarn and fabric imports were nearly flat, while exports showed marginal shifts.
Textile machinery exports remained strong, signalling resilience in Japan’s industrial segment.

title: Kering to propose Chenut & Kleitman as independent directors at AGM
summary:
Kering will propose Marie-Hélène Chenut and Laurent Kleitman as independent directors at its May 28, 2026 AGM. 
Jean-Pierre Denis will step down after 18 years. 
Chairman François-Henri Pinault praised his contribution. 
If approved, the board will have 13 members, 64% independent, 45% women, and 5 nationalities, strengthening global expertise.

title: Bangladesh, Ethiopia discuss boosting trade, investment cooperation
summary:
Bangladesh and Ethiopia recently stressed the need to strengthen bilateral ties, focusing on trade, investment, and sectoral cooperation, as the former’s Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman met the latter’s President Taye Atskeselassie Made in Addis Ababa. 
They discussed ways to enhance cooperation in trade, investment, education and agriculture. 
Welcomed investment from Bangladesh in Ethiopia.
